state.output:
<path>/private/var/folders/9f/txt0j2rs2wx3lqx9vfj9x3240000gn/T/BenchmarkRenameqwen3.5-9b-udq4boundary_687dd1bdraw719921847/004/boundary/internal/servers/controller/session_cleanup_job.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package controller
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	stderrors "errors"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"time"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/observability/event"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/scheduler"
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/servers/controller/common"
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/session"
14: )
15: 
16: // sessionConnectionCleanupJob defines a periodic job that monitors workers for
17: // loss of connection and terminates connections on workers that have
18: // not sent a heartbeat in a significant period of time.
19: //
20: // Relevant connections are simply marked as disconnected in the
21: // database. Connections will be independently terminated by the
22: // worker, or the event of a synchronization issue between the two,
23: // the controller will win out and order that the connections be
24: // closed on the worker.
25: type sessionConnectionCleanupJob struct {
26: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory
27: 
28: 	// The amount of time to give disconnected workers before marking
29: 	// their connections as closed.
30: 	gracePeriod time.Duration
31: 
32: 	// The total number of connections closed in the last run.
33: 	totalClosed int
34: }
35: 
36: // newSessionConnectionCleanupJob instantiates the session cleanup job.
37: func newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(
38: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory,
39: 	gracePeriod time.Duration,
40: ) (*sessionConnectionCleanupJob, error) {
41: 	const op = "controller.newNewSessionConnectionCleanupJob"
42: 	switch {
43: 	case connectionRepoFn == nil:
44: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing connectionRepoFn")
45: 	case gracePeriod < session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace:
46: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(
47: 			errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("invalid gracePeriod, must be greater than %s", session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace))
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	return &sessionConnectionCleanupJob{
51: 		connectionRepoFn: connectionRepoFn,
52: 		gracePeriod:      gracePeriod,
53: 	}, nil
54: }
55: 
56: // Name returns a short, unique name for the job.
57: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Name() string { return "session_cleanup" }
58: 
59: // Description returns the description for the job.
60: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Description() string {
61: 	return "Clean up session connections from disconnected workers"
62: }
63: 
64: // NextRunIn returns the next run time after a job is completed.
65: //
66: // The next run time is defined for sessionConnectionCleanupJob as one second.
67: // This is because the job should run continuously to terminate
68: // connections as soon as a worker has not reported in for a long
69: // enough time. Only one job will ever run at once, so there is no
70: // reason why it cannot run again immediately.
71: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
72: 	return time.Second, nil
73: }
74: 
75: // Status returns the status of the running job.
76: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {
77: 	return scheduler.JobStatus{
78: 		Completed: j.totalClosed,
79: 		Total:     j.totalClosed,
80: 	}
81: }
82: 
83: // Run executes the job.
84: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
85: 	const op = "controller.(sessionConnectionCleanupJob).Run"
86: 	j.totalClosed = 0
87: 
88: 	// Load repos.
89: 	connectionRepo, err := j.connectionRepoFn()
90: 	if err != nil {
91: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error getting session repo"))
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	// Run the atomic dead worker cleanup job.
95: 	results, err := connectionRepo.CloseConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, j.gracePeriod)
96: 	if err != nil {
97: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	for _, result := range results {
101: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("worker has not reported status within acceptable grace period, all connections closed"),
102: 			event.WithInfo(
103: 				"private_id", result.ServerId,
104: 				"update_time", result.LastUpdateTime,
105: 				"grace_period_seconds", j.gracePeriod.Seconds(),
106: 				"number_connections_closed", result.NumberConnectionsClosed,
107: 			))
108: 
109: 		j.totalClosed += result.NumberConnectionsClosed
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	return nil
113: }

(End of file - total 113 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package controller
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"testing"
7: 	"time"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/db"
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/iam"
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms"
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/scheduler"
14: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/servers"
15: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/session"
16: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
17: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
18: )
19: 
20: // assert the interface
21: var _ = scheduler.Job(new(sessionConnectionCleanupJob))
22: 
23: // This test has been largely adapted from
24: // TestRepository_CloseDeadConnectionsOnWorker in
25: // internal/session/repository_connection_test.go.
26: func TestSessionConnectionCleanupJob(t *testing.T) {
27: 	t.Parallel()
28: 	ctx := context.Background()
29: 
30: 	const gracePeriod = 1 * time.Second
31: 
32: 	require, assert := require.New(t), assert.New(t)
33: 	conn, _ := db.TestSetup(t, "postgres")
34: 	rw := db.New(conn)
35: 	wrapper := db.TestWrapper(t)
36: 	iamRepo := iam.TestRepo(t, conn, wrapper)
37: 	kms := kms.TestKms(t, conn, wrapper)
38: 	serversRepo, err := servers.NewRepository(rw, rw, kms)
39: 	require.NoError(err)
40: 	sessionRepo, err := session.NewRepository(rw, rw, kms)
41: 	connectionRepo, err := session.NewConnectionRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kms, session.WithDeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace(gracePeriod))
42: 	require.NoError(err)
43: 
44: 	numConns := 12
45: 
46: 	// Create two "workers". One will remain untouched while the other "goes
47: 	// away and comes back" (worker 2).
48: 	worker1 := session.TestWorker(t, conn, wrapper, session.WithServerId("worker1"))
49: 	worker2 := session.TestWorker(t, conn, wrapper, session.WithServerId("worker2"))
50: 
51: 	// Create a few sessions on each, activate, and authorize a connection
52: 	var connIds []string
53: 	connIdsByWorker := make(map[string][]string)
54: 	for i := 0; i < numConns; i++ {
55: 		serverId := worker1.PrivateId
56: 		if i%2 == 0 {
57: 			serverId = worker2.PrivateId
58: 		}
59: 		sess := session.TestDefaultSession(t, conn, wrapper, iamRepo, session.WithServerId(serverId), session.WithDbOpts(db.WithSkipVetForWrite(true)))
60: 		sess, _, err = sessionRepo.ActivateSession(ctx, sess.GetPublicId(), sess.Version, serverId, "worker", []byte("foo"))
61: 		require.NoError(err)
62: 		c, cs, _, err := session.AuthorizeConnection(ctx, sessionRepo, connectionRepo, sess.GetPublicId(), serverId)
63: 		require.NoError(err)
64: 		require.Len(cs, 1)
65: 		require.Equal(session.StatusAuthorized, cs[0].Status)
66: 		connIds = append(connIds, c.GetPublicId())
67: 		if i%2 == 0 {
68: 			connIdsByWorker[worker2.PrivateId] = append(connIdsByWorker[worker2.PrivateId], c.GetPublicId())
69: 		} else {
70: 			connIdsByWorker[worker1.PrivateId] = append(connIdsByWorker[worker1.PrivateId], c.GetPublicId())
71: 		}
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	// Mark half of the connections connected and leave the others authorized.
75: 	// This is just to ensure we have a spread when we test it out.
76: 	for i, connId := range connIds {
77: 		if i%2 == 0 {
78: 			_, cs, err := connectionRepo.ConnectConnection(ctx, session.ConnectWith{
79: 				ConnectionId:       connId,
80: 				ClientTcpAddress:   "127.0.0.1",
81: 				ClientTcpPort:      22,
82: 				EndpointTcpAddress: "127.0.0.1",
83: 				EndpointTcpPort:    22,
84: 				UserClientIp:       "127.0.0.1",
85: 			})
86: 			require.NoError(err)
87: 			require.Len(cs, 2)
88: 			var foundAuthorized, foundConnected bool
89: 			for _, status := range cs {
90: 				if status.Status == session.StatusAuthorized {
91: 					foundAuthorized = true
92: 				}
93: 				if status.Status == session.StatusConnected {
94: 					foundConnected = true
95: 				}
96: 			}
97: 			require.True(foundAuthorized)
98: 			require.True(foundConnected)
99: 		}
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	// Create the job.
103: 	job, err := newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(
104: 		func() (*session.ConnectionRepository, error) { return connectionRepo, nil },
105: 		session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace,
106: 	)
107: 	job.gracePeriod = gracePeriod // by-pass factory assert so we dont have to wait so long
108: 	require.NoError(err)
109: 
110: 	// sleep the status grace period.
111: 	time.Sleep(gracePeriod)
112: 
113: 	// Push an upsert to the first worker so that its status has been
114: 	// updated.
115: 	_, rowsUpdated, err := serversRepo.UpsertServer(ctx, worker1, []servers.Option{}...)
116: 	require.NoError(err)
117: 	require.Equal(1, rowsUpdated)
118: 
119: 	// Run the job.
120: 	require.NoError(job.Run(ctx))
121: 
122: 	// Assert connection state on both workers.
123: 	assertConnections := func(workerId string, closed bool) {
124: 		connIds, ok := connIdsByWorker[workerId]
125: 		require.True(ok)
126: 		require.Len(connIds, 6)
127: 		for _, connId := range connIds {
128: 			_, states, err := connectionRepo.LookupConnection(ctx, connId, nil)
129: 			require.NoError(err)
130: 			var foundClosed bool
131: 			for _, state := range states {
132: 				if state.Status == session.StatusClosed {
133: 					foundClosed = true
134: 					break
135: 				}
136: 			}
137: 			assert.Equal(closed, foundClosed)
138: 		}
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	// Assert that all connections on the second worker are closed
142: 	assertConnections(worker2.PrivateId, true)
143: 	// Assert that all connections on the first worker are still open
144: 	assertConnections(worker1.PrivateId, false)
145: }
146: 
147: func TestSessionConnectionCleanupJobNewJobErr(t *testing.T) {
148: 	t.Parallel()
149: 	ctx := context.TODO()
150: 	const op = "controller.newNewSessionConnectionCleanupJob"
151: 	require := require.New(t)
152: 
153: 	job, err := newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(nil, 0)
154: 	require.Equal(err, errors.E(
155: 		ctx,
156: 		errors.WithCode(errors.InvalidParameter),
157: 		errors.WithOp(op),
158: 		errors.WithMsg("missing connectionRepoFn"),
159: 	))
160: 	require.Nil(job)
161: 
162: 	job, err = newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(func() (*session.ConnectionRepository, error) { return nil, nil }, 0)
163: 	require.Equal(err, errors.E(
164: 		ctx,
165: 		errors.WithCode(errors.InvalidParameter),
166: 		errors.WithOp(op),
167: 		errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("invalid gracePeriod, must be greater than %s", session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace)),
168: 	))
169: 	require.Nil(job)
170: }

(End of file - total 170 lines)
</content>
